yaml.title: Claude Code for HGIS-to-LINCS
yaml.subtitle: A hands-on workshop for historians converting historical GIS data to Linked Open Data

markdown.markdown:
This workshop teaches you to use [Claude Code](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code) --- Anthropic's command-line AI assistant --- to work with Historical GIS data and convert it into [LINCS](https://lincsproject.ca/)-compatible Linked Open Data. No programming experience is required.

By the end of this session, you will have:

1. **Used Claude Code** to explore, clean, and analyze GIS data through conversation
2. **Learned to give effective instructions** to an AI coding assistant
3. **Loaded LINCS-specific skills** that give Claude deep knowledge of CIDOC-CRM and the LINCS application profile
4. **Converted sample HGIS data** to LINCS-compatible RDF/Turtle

## Why Claude Code?

Most GenAI tools (ChatGPT, Gemini) work through a web browser. Claude Code is different. It runs in your terminal and can directly read, write, and edit files on your computer. This makes it far more useful for data work:

- It reads your CSV, GeoJSON, and Shapefile attribute tables directly
- It writes output files to your project folder
- It runs Python, R, or shell commands on your behalf
- It remembers project context across a session
- It can be extended with **skills** --- specialized knowledge packages that make it an expert in specific domains

## Before the Workshop

Please complete the **[Installation](installation.qmd)** steps before arriving. This includes creating an Anthropic account and installing Claude Code (or signing up for Codex as a browser-based alternative). If you get stuck, bring your laptop and we will help you at the start of the session.

## Workshop Schedule

### Part 1: Working with GIS Data (0:00--1:00)

| Time | Section |
|------|---------|
| 0:00 | Troubleshoot remaining installations |
| 0:10 | [What Is Claude Code?](what-is-claude-code.qmd) --- orientation and key concepts |
| 0:20 | [Data Ethics and Privacy](data-ethics.qmd) --- OCAP, sensitive data, and what not to share |
| 0:30 | [Your First Session](first-session.qmd) --- guided walkthrough |
| 0:40 | [GIS Data Exercises](gis-exercises.qmd) --- exploring and analyzing HGIS data with Claude Code |

### Break (1:00--1:10)

### Part 2: Linked Open Data (1:10--2:00)

| Time | Section |
|------|---------|
| 1:10 | [LINCS Skills](lincs-skills.qmd) --- loading domain expertise for LOD work |
| 1:30 | [HGIS-to-LINCS Exercise](hands-on.qmd) --- converting GIS data to CIDOC-CRM / RDF |
| 1:50 | Discussion, questions, and next steps |
